subject: Project Management
title: Queuing Theory
description: A short lesson to help you apply Queuing Theory.

hook.text: At 70% utilization, wait times are manageable. At 90%, they explode exponentially.

buildup.text: Queuing theory, developed for telephone networks, explains why overloaded systems collapse.

discovery.text: The math shows that keeping some slack — unused capacity — is essential for fast flow.

twist.text: Managers who demand 100% utilization actually slow everything down. Slack is not waste.

climax.text: Kanban's WIP limits are queuing theory in action: don't overload the system.

quiz.question: Why does operating at 100% utilization slow systems down?
options: Wait times explode exponentially without slack
Workers refuse to work at full capacity
